name: Maldives

colors.symbolism: the bravery of the Maldivian heroes who gave their lives defending the nation
life, prosperity, peace, and the palm trees that grow on the islands

description: A red field bearing a large green rectangle in the centre — the red forming a border around the green panel of width one-quarter of the flag's height. Centred on the green panel, a white crescent moon opens toward the fly.
history: Adopted on 25 July 1965, the day of independence from the United Kingdom. The current design replaced an earlier flag that had also borne a black-and-white striped panel along the hoist; this hoist panel was dropped in 1953 when the Maldives briefly became a republic, and the current crescent (without the earlier vertical stripes inside it) was finalised at independence.

features.symbolism: a red border surrounding a green panel; red for the bravery of the nation's heroes who sacrificed their lives for the country, green for prosperity, peace, and palm trees

features.role: white crescent moon (opening toward the fly)
features.symbolism: Islam, the state religion of the Maldives
